earnedTime.heading: Understanding Your Release Dates & Earned Time
earnedTime.body:
## Understanding Your Release Dates

Colorado tracks two main dates that determine when you can be released from prison. Both dates can move earlier each month as you earn Earned Time credits.

### Parole Eligibility Date (PED)
The earliest date you can be considered for release to parole supervision. Reaching this date does not guarantee release, the Parole Board will review your case and decide whether to grant parole.

### Mandatory Release Date (MRD)
The latest date you must be released to parole.

## Earned Time Credits

Earned Time is the primary way to reduce your time in prison. It is awarded monthly based on your participation and progress.

###How is Earned Time awarded?
Each month, you can earn up to 10 days of regular Earned Time across three categories:

| Credit Type             | Max Amount                    | How to Earn It                                                                                                                   |
| :---------------------- | :---------------------------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Work / Training         | 4 days/month                  | Active participation with a “Fair” or better work rating                                                                         |
| Group Living            | 2 days/month                  | Positive behavior in your housing unit                                                                                           |
| Counseling / Programs   | 4 days/month                  | Active participation in assigned programs                                                                                        |
| Provisional Earned Time | Up to 2 additional days/month | Available if you are serving a Class 4, 5, or 6 felony (or Level 3–4 drug felony) with no recent serious disciplinary violations |

You can also earn one-time bonus credits for completing specific milestones, such as completing a GED, vocational program, or therapeutic phase.

### When do I not earn Earned Time?
You will not earn Earned Time for that month if:
  - You receive a work rating below “Fair”
  - You refuse to participate in required programs
  - You receive a **Class I disciplinary conviction**
      - You are ineligible for the month of conviction plus the next 2 months
  - You receive a **Class II disciplinary conviction**
      - You are ineligible for the month of conviction plus the next 1 month

yosEarnedTime.heading: Earn Discretionary Transition Time (DTT) and Move to Your Next Phase Faster
yosEarnedTime.body:
## Your Phase Journey

| Phase   | Name                                | What it means                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Key note                               |
| :------ | :---------------------------------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | :------------------------------------- |
| **I**   | **Institutional**                   | You earn DTT every month through programs, good behavior, and staying on track. You can earn additional 3 months in Phase II. By participating in programs, maintaining good behavior, and staying on track, you accumulate DTT monthly. Earning more DTT accelerates your eligibility for Phase II                                                                                    | **Earn up to 3 DTT days/month**        |
| **II**  | **Phase II Eligibility**            | Earliest day you’re able to move to Phase II. Your actual date depends on your choices and behavior but you can earn it up to 3 months earlier than your mandatory date by keeping a clean record and earning all your DTT. The earliest day that you can transition to Phase II is up to 6 months prior to your Phase III date, based on the number of DTT days earned.               | **Good behavior = Earlier date**       |
| **III** | **Community (living outside)**      | A minimum of six months will be spent in the community, you can earn additional six months based on earned DTT days. Your actual date will depend on your choices and behavior. Your Phase III date is set 3 to 6 months after you earn your Phase II date. You are still subject to suitability hearing for the entirety of the community phase.                                      | **6 months - 1 year in the Community** |
| **D**   | **Discharge (going home for good)** | Once phase III–the community portion–is completed, your sentence is completely done and the suspended adult sentence will go away. The completion of the program includes your choices and behavior while in the community. Then you are off supervision and no longer owe the State of Colorado Department of Corrections time. This means no one is monitoring you after this point. | **No Parole Tail**                     |

## How You Earn DTT Days

You can earn up to 3 DTT days every month. There are three separate categories — you can earn 1 day in each:

| Days  | Category                     | What counts                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| :---- | :--------------------------- | :----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **1** | **School or job training**   | Showing up on time, participating in program assignments, having positive interactions with staff and peers, equipment accountability, and maintaining good to fair performance evaluations.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| **1** | **Program participation**    | Actively participating in required programs like: GED, Anger Management, Drug and Alcohol education, Mental Health support. Program attendance and appropriate participation will determine if you earn a DTT day. If you participate in mental health or GED programming while in the Classification Unit, you may still be eligible for a DTT day. To earn a DTT day for behavioral health programs, overall program compliance is required. Compliance factors can include attendance and participation in group and individual therapy, family therapy, taking your medications as prescribed, attending your medical and psychiatry appointments, and more. If you have questions about what your specific compliance factors are, submit a kite to medical or mental health and they will provide you with more details. |
| **1** | **How you live on the unit** | This covers your daily behavior: keeping your area clean, good hygiene, being respectful and positive toward staff and other residents, being productive and participating in group activities                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |

## Bonus days for Big Achievements

On top of the 3 days you can earn every month, completing certain programs earns you extra bonus days called Achievement or Milestone days. These are one-time credits — you earn them once when you finish the program. Every one of these is worth working toward.

| Program or activity                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Bonus days earned                             |
| :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | :-------------------------------------------- |
| GED (high school equivalency diploma) earned at YOS                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Milestone days (confirm exact amount) 3 Days  |
| College class credits: you earn 1 bonus day for every college class you complete                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | **1 day per class completed**                 |
| Associate Degree (two-year college degree): completing the full degree earns an extra bonus on top of the per-class credits                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | **3 days**                                    |
| OTP Honor Graduate: OTP stands for Orientation and Training Phase, the overall YOS program. Graduating as an Honor Graduate means you completed the program at the highest level of achievement.                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Milestone days (*confirm exact amount*) 1 Day |
| Re-entry classes: practical certifications that help you get a job when you leave. Examples include: OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health, a safety certificate employers look for), and Gladiator (job readiness training).                                                                                                                                                                       | Milestone days (*confirm exact amount*) 1 Day |
| PCC Mobile Labs: Pueblo Community College classes brought on-site to YOS. Completing a lab session earns bonus days.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Milestone days (*confirm exact amount*)       |
| Apprenticeship programs 600 hours of hands-on job training in Graphic Design. An apprenticeship shows future employers you have real, documented work experience.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Milestone days (*confirm exact amount*)       |
| Cognitive Education class: classes that teach you how thinking patterns affect your choices and how to make better decisions. Cognitive Education class: A course designed to help you understand how your thought patterns influence your choices and equip you with tools for better decision-making                                                                                             | **1 day per class completed**                 |
| Seven Habits on the Inside: a program based on the book The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People, adapted for the YOS setting. It covers goal-setting, responsibility, and planning for your future.                                                                                                                                                                                                | Milestone days (*confirm exact amount*) 1 Day |
| CTE (Career and Technical Education) credits: CTE courses give you industry-recognized certificates in skilled trades and technology fields. Up to 3 credits are available per area. Available areas include: Graphic Design, Computer Information Systems (tech and IT), Customer Service, Foundations of Career Tech, Introduction to Carpentry, Welding, Automotive, and Business Fundamentals. | **Up to 3 credits available per area**        |

## How you Become Ineligible to Earn DTT Days

**How you stop earning DTT days**

If you receive a Class I or Class II COPD (a formal disciplinary write-up — Class I is more serious, Class II is less serious but still counts) during a month, you will not be able to earn any DTT days for that month.

Ask your Program Supervisor if you have questions about how this works.

Receiving a Class I or Class II COPD (formal disciplinary write-up) during any month will forfeit your ability to earn DTT days for that entire month.

Although Class I write-ups are more serious than Class II, either infraction will impact your eligibility.

You are only eligible for milestone DTT during the month of your conviction

## How to Earn Your Phase II Date 6 Months Early

**Earn your Phase II date up to 6 months early**

If you earn 100% of your possible DTT days (all 3 days every month, with no write-ups) you could move to Phase II up to 3 months earlier than your mandatory date. That means you could move to Phase III Community 6 months sooner too. Every month of full DTT and prosocial behavior gets you closer to that earlier date.

You can progress to Phase II of the YOS program up to 3 months earlier than your mandatory date. To earn this, you must earn 100% or more of your possible DTT days (all 3 days every month) at the time of your Placement Committee review.

Remember, every month of full DTT and good behavior gets you closer to an earlier release date.

**Phase dates will be determined at your placement committee meeting. Questions about your DTT percentage can be addressed at the monthly Progress Team Review (PTR) meetings.**

programs.lastUpdated: Program information was last updated {{date, formatFullDate}}.
programs.pageDescription:
This page helps you find programs and activities that earn you Achievement Credits. These credits can take time off your sentence. You can earn a total of 120 days of credit. The list updates from time to time – look for the "New" label to see what has changed. Ask your Case Manager if you qualify for a particular program, and to help you sign up.

YOS offenders are not eligible for standard achievement earned time awards, but will continue to earn Discretionary Time (DT) days in accordance with YOS policy and practice.

mrdTent.label: Mandatory Release Date (MRD)
mrdTent.description: The date you must be released to parole if you have not been granted discretionary parole earlier.

pedTent.label: Parole Eligibility Date (PED)
pedTent.description: The earliest date you are eligible for discretionary release to parole.
